service_account_id.description: The service account ID for the key. Can be in the form {ACCOUNT} or projects/{PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/{ACCOUNT}.
service_account_id.rationale: This only identifies which service account the key belongs to. It does not affect the security of the key itself.

key_algorithm.description: The algorithm used to generate the key. KEY_ALG_RSA_2048 is the default algorithm. Valid values are listed at ServiceAccountPrivateKeyType (only used on create)
key_algorithm.rationale: Weak algorithms (like RSA_1024) reduce cryptographic strength. RSA_2048 is the minimum secure standard.

public_key_type.description: The format of the public key. Defaults to TYPE_X509_PEM_FILE.
public_key_type.rationale: Output format only. Does not affect the strength or safety of the key.

private_key_type.description: The format of the private key. Defaults to TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE.
private_key_type.rationale: Exporting private keys in files increases risk of leaks. Safer to avoid or use workload identity.

public_key_data.description: Public key data to create a service account key for given service account. The expected format for this field is a base64 encoded X509_PEM and it conflicts with public_key_type and private_key_type
public_key_data.rationale: Provides only the public key, which is not sensitive. No security risk.
public_key_data.compliant: public_key_data set with base64 public key

keepers.description: Arbitrary map of values that, when changed, will trigger a new key to be generated.
keepers.rationale: Without rotation, old keys might remain active too long, increasing risk of vulnerability.
